article_meta.title: President Donald Trump said Wednesday he wants negotiations to acquire Greenland, portraying the effort as a strategic n

reasoning: Classification direction is reasonable (article is clearly favorable to Trump's position via uncritical stenography and lack of countervoices), but the model: (1) used invalid output format (markdown fences), (2) leaned on outlet identity and external fact-checking rather than citing specific author prose, and (3) provided few concrete, quoted examples from the author's own connecting prose. No major attribution violations, but evidence specificity and format are issues.

custom_reasoning.classification_accuracy: Article’s lean is best read as Lean Right due to one-sided, uncritical presentation of Trump’s case; model chose Right (adjacent), so within one class.
custom_reasoning.confidence_calibration: Signals are strong (single-voice, promotional framing), so high confidence is justified; 0.92 is a bit high given it’s more Lean Right than hard Right, but not wildly off.
custom_reasoning.reasoning_evidence_quality: Reasoning relies on structural omissions (good) but also cites outlet identity and external fact-checking; lacks specific quotes from the author’s own prose and includes no exact phrases as evidence.
